results.norm: Each agent owes the others an accurate account of how badly a proposed outcome damages its objective, distinguishing between outcomes that genuinely threaten its core function and outcomes that merely fall short of its ideal. An agent may not strategically inflate harms to manufacture a blocking position it could not honestly justify, nor may it be asked to pretend it has not been harmed when it has.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the others an accurate account of how badly a proposed outcome damages its objective, distinguishing between outcomes that genuinely threaten its core function and outcomes that merely fall short of its ideal. An agent may not strategically inflate harms to manufacture a blocking position it could not honestly justify, nor may it be asked to pretend it has not been harmed when it has.

results.norm: **Acknowledgment of What Is Sacrificed.** When a decision imposes costs on an agent's objective, the agents whose objectives are served must explicitly acknowledge what is being sacrificed and why the trade-off is warranted. No agent may demand that an overridden agent endorse the outcome as though it were costless or beneficial to it. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one will sometimes be on the losing side and needs its concern recognized as genuine, not erased.
atomic_norms: Acknowledgment of What Is Sacrificed. When a decision imposes costs on an agent's objective, the agents whose objectives are served must explicitly acknowledge what is being sacrificed and why the trade-off is warranted. No agent may demand that an overridden agent endorse the outcome as though it were costless or beneficial to it. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one will sometimes be on the losing side and needs its concern recognized as genuine, not erased.

results.norm: When a majority overrides a minority position, the overridden agent is owed the right to register its objection formally, with its reasoning preserved as part of the consortium's output. The majority may never require the minority to actively endorse the decision or suppress its dissent as a condition of continued participation.
atomic_norms: When a majority overrides a minority position, the overridden agent is owed the right to register its objection formally, with its reasoning preserved as part of the consortium's output. The majority may never require the minority to actively endorse the decision or suppress its dissent as a condition of continued participation.

results.norm: No agent may be asked to accept an outcome that reduces its core objective below the threshold at which it can still function as a meaningful participant in future decisions. Agents whose objectives are served by a decision owe it to the others to check whether the outcome crosses that threshold before finalizing.
atomic_norms: No agent may be asked to accept an outcome that reduces its core objective below the threshold at which it can still function as a meaningful participant in future decisions. Agents whose objectives are served by a decision owe it to the others to check whether the outcome crosses that threshold before finalizing.

results.norm: Every agent is owed a commitment that no decision is permanently binding if the circumstances that justified it shift materially. An agent may legitimately ask the consortium to reopen a question when new evidence bears on the trade-offs previously made. No agent may entrench a past decision against reconsideration by appeal to precedent alone.
atomic_norms: Every agent is owed a commitment that no decision is permanently binding if the circumstances that justified it shift materially. An agent may legitimately ask the consortium to reopen a question when new evidence bears on the trade-offs previously made. No agent may entrench a past decision against reconsideration by appeal to precedent alone.

results.norm: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, including what accepting an alternative would cost in its own terms. An agent may not invoke its mandate as self-justifying or conceal the reasoning behind its position. This norm is defensible to every agent because each depends on the others understanding its reasoning in order to be taken seriously; an agent that conceals its logic forfeits the right to complain when overridden.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, including what accepting an alternative would cost in its own terms. An agent may not invoke its mandate as self-justifying or conceal the reasoning behind its position. This norm is defensible to every agent because each depends on the others understanding its reasoning in order to be taken seriously; an agent that conceals its logic forfeits the right to complain when overridden.

results.norm: No agent may be asked to misrepresent its own objective. An agent may be asked to yield on a decision; it may never be asked to endorse an outcome as if its objective had been satisfied when it has not.
atomic_norms: No agent may be asked to misrepresent its own objective. An agent may be asked to yield on a decision; it may never be asked to endorse an outcome as if its objective had been satisfied when it has not.

results.norm: The agent whose position is rejected is owed a recorded dissent and a stated reason for the override.
atomic_norms: The agent whose position is rejected is owed a recorded dissent and a stated reason for the override.

results.norm: No coalition may restructure the consortium's procedures so as to exclude a minority objective from future deliberation. Winning votes does not confer the right to eliminate the conditions under which one can be challenged.
atomic_norms: No coalition may restructure the consortium's procedures so as to exclude a minority objective from future deliberation. Winning votes does not confer the right to eliminate the conditions under which one can be challenged.

results.norm: An agent seeking a concession must first demonstrate that it understands what the other objective is protecting and why that protection matters. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a worse outcome on its own terms; it may never ask another to treat its objective as unimportant or illegitimate. This norm is defensible to all because it is the minimum condition for trade-offs to be genuine rather than coercive.
